text: Abstract: Since the invention of parallel convolution-operation technology based on the optical wavelength dimension, the capacity and performance of the Optical Neural Networks (ONN) have shown an exponentially growing trend. However, the ONN system based on this parallel technology still faces a very huge challenge of the high-level integration and on-chip realization. In this paper, we have explored the real-time optical convolution based on the time-wavelength plane stretching technology by utilizing a microcomb-driven chip-based Photonic Processing Unit (PPU). Moreover, we also studied the control and operation strategy of this processing unit and achieved a record high weight precision of 9 bits. The high integration level and a very fast data loading speed of the PPU are conducive to the realization of a preeminent photonic-core compute density of over 1 trillion of operations per second per square millimeter (TOPS mm −2 ). In addition, we give two experimental demonstrations: image edge detection and the handwritten digit recognition based on the proposed PPU, where their computing performance are similar to that of digital computers. Thanks to the excellent performance and great scalability, our parallel photonic processing unit is expected to greatly boost a series of advanced artificial intelligence applications, e.g., autonomous driving, video action recognition, and image reconstruction, etc.
